papers.name: Paper 3 Theory (Core)
papers.coverage_notes: Core syllabus. Short-answer and structured questions.

papers.name: Paper 4 Theory (Extended)
papers.coverage_notes: Extended syllabus (Core + Supplement). Short-answer and structured questions.

papers.name: Paper 5 Practical Test
papers.coverage_notes: Hands-on practical test at an external exam centre. Required for IGCSE award.

papers.name: Paper 6 Alternative to Practical
papers.coverage_notes: Written alternative for centres unable to run Paper 5.

assessment_objectives.name: Knowledge with understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Recall and demonstrate understanding of scientific facts, concepts, principles and theories.
classification_hints: Items asking for definitions, structures, processes, terminology, named facts.

assessment_objectives.name: Handling information and problem-solving
assessment_objectives.description: Apply biological knowledge to familiar and unfamiliar situations; interpret and analyse data.
classification_hints: Items involving graph/chart reading, calculation, interpretation of experimental data.

assessment_objectives.name: Experimental skills and investigations
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate experimental skills including planning, observation, recording, analysis and evaluation.
classification_hints: Items involving practical procedure, apparatus, variables, controls, safety, evaluation of methods.

question_styles.name: Multiple choice
question_styles.description: Tests recall and basic application across the syllabus.

question_styles.name: Structured question
question_styles.description: Multi-part questions requiring analysis and application.

question_styles.name: Practical / Alternative to practical
question_styles.description: Experimental design, data handling, evaluation of methods.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions or single-step application.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when a teacher/student/parent mode is declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: CAIE IGCSE Biology command words (state, describe, explain, discuss, calculate, plan, suggest) are defined in Section 4 of the syllabus.
marking_guidance.notes: Tier-aware marking: Core tier caps at grade C; Extended tier allows A*-G. Practical (Paper 5/6) is a separate paper graded independently.
